mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utilities output, indicating higher aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue growth that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases do not outperform those following decreases by at least 100bps annualized over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy increases borrowing costs and slows credit-sensitive spending, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and rising unemployment captures a supply-demand deterioration that single indicators miss, amplifying downside earnings risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal defined by INDPRO declining and UNRATE rising fails to produce negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered months during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, declining housing starts indicate that elevated interest rates are curtailing construction and durable goods demand, foreshadowing weaker aggregate demand and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns following below-median HOUST readings are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or are less negative than in low-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and consumption capacity, which supports higher corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive monthly change in PAYEMS does not coincide with positive average 20-day S&P 500 forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising more than 0.3 percentage points above its trailing 12-month minimum marks the end of labor-market slack absorption, triggering recession risk repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE triggers the 0.3pp threshold crossing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL above 0.3% signals upside inflation surprises that prompt near-term repricing of terminal rate expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following CPIAUCSL acceleration > 0.3% are not negative in at least 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth above its trailing median captures broad-based aggregate demand expansion that reduces recession probability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth above its 5-year median does not correspond to positive average 60-day S&P 500 forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.
